dataset: CT Cranium DICOM sample (0051.zip from InVesalius)

pi_items.source: Found exactly matched in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh variables.
Enforced by verifier.py. Verified via Python and skimage.measure.marching_cubes on the DICOM data that the actual left hemicranium has ~941k triangles, easily exceeding 10000.

Enforced by verifier.py. Computed true bounding box of left hemicranium from DICOM data using Python (~112.45mm width, 214.37mm length, 210.00mm height) which yields a ratio of ~0.52, fitting within the < 0.60 requirement.
Enforced by verifier.py. Python analysis of DICOM pixel spacing and image boundaries confirmed the true skull length is ~214.37mm.
Enforced by verifier.py. Python analysis of DICOM slices confirmed the true skull height is ~210.00mm.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a real public CT Cranium DICOM sample (0051.zip) downloaded directly from the InVesalius GitHub repository. The required output file path '/home/ga/Documents/left_hemicranium.stl' exactly matches the setup and export scripts. The geometric constraints defining a valid left hemicranium (min 10000 triangles, max width/length ratio 0.60, min length 120.0mm, min height 100.0mm) were confirmed by manually processing the DICOM data via Python scripts: the actual hemicranium mesh yields ~941k triangles, a width/length ratio of ~0.52 (112.45mm / 214.37mm), a length of ~214.37mm, and a height of ~210.00mm, strictly satisfying all verifier thresholds.
